dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7125 IH: Stop Body Camera Paywalls Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Tlaib (for herself, Ms. Clarke of New York, Mr. Espaillat, Mr. Evans of Pennsylvania, Mr. García of Illinois, Mrs. Grijalva, Mr. Johnson of Georgia, Ms. Lee of Pennsylvania, Mrs. McIver, Mr. Norcross, Ms. Norton, Ms. Omar, Ms. Pressley, Mrs. Ramirez, Ms. Simon, Ms. Stansbury, and Ms. Velázquez) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit charging for access to certain camera video footage.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stop Body Camera Paywalls Act.

section.header: Providing access to certain camera footage free of charge

subsection.text: An application by a State or a unit of local government for funding under a covered program shall include a certification that such State, unit of local government, and any law enforcement agency to which the State or unit of local government will distribute funds, does not impose any financial or monetary cost on any member of the public seeking to view, access, or obtain a copy of certain camera video footage recorded by a law enforcement officer, or impose any other fees related to obtaining certain camera footage, including court fees.
A State or unit of local government shall be ineligible for funding under a covered program without providing the certification described under subsection (a).

paragraph.text: The term Byrne grant program means any grant program under subpart 1 of part E of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10151 et seq.), without regard to whether the funds are characterized as being made available under the Edward Byrne Memorial State and Local Law Enforcement Assistance Programs, the Local Government Law Enforcement Block Grants Program, the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program, or otherwise.
The term certain camera video footage includes—

subparagraph.text: body camera video footage recorded by a law enforcement officer;
dash camera video footage recorded by a law enforcement officer;
jail, prison, or correctional facility surveillance footage; or
any other relevant law enforcement agency footage.

paragraph.text: The term COPS grant program means the grant program authorized under section 1701 of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10381).
The term covered program means any program or activity funded in whole or in part with funds made available under—
